calendar.matters:
Petitioner Donald Trammel By And Through His Gal Clinton Killians Petition To Remove Trustee Lawrence Porchia And Appointment Of Clinton Killian As Trustee Ad Litem
Off Calendar Jun-22-2021 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Reforming, Or Alternately Modifying The Terms Of The Trust Or In The Alternative Modifying The Trust Or In The Alternative Seeking
Off Calendar Jun-22-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Brown, via ZOOM, for Floyd Trammell; Christina Morgan and Loan Dao, via ZOOM, for Kilby Stenkamp, Respondent; Kendall Layne, via CourtCall, for Tracy Dearman, NorCal Gold and Fusion Real Estate, Respondents; Nader Fannyan, via ZOOM, for Donald Trammell, Petitioner; Hussein Saffouri, via ZOOM, for the Trammell Estate.

The court begins by confirming with counsel that the two petitions in this matter can be taken off calendar. No objections are presented.

The Petition for Order Reforming, etc., and Petition to Remove, etc., in this matter are taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Neves. Not reported.

Petition For Order Reforming, Or Alternately Modifying The Terms Of The Trust Or In The Alternative Modifying The Trust Or In The Alternative Seeking
Off Calendar Feb-23-2021 Continued To Jun-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-17-301488 Estate of Genette Trammell and
PTR-18-302129 The Genette Trammell Living Trust.

Appearances: Jennifer Brown and Jose Cordova appeared by zoom for Floyd Trammell.
Christina Morgan appeared by zoom for Kilby Stenkamp (Respondents).
Nader Fannyan appeared by zoom for Clinton Killian (Guardian Ad Litem for Donald Trammell).
Kendall Layne appeared by zoom for Norcal Gold, Fusion Real Estate Network and Tracy Dearman (Respondents).
Christianne Hipps appeared in court for and with Lawrence Porchia in court.
Mark Wilson appeared by zoom for Vanguard Properties and Kilby Stenkamp (Respondents).

The Court stated that status of mediation is off calendar and that the competing petitions for probate are going to be set for trial. Ms. Hipps stated that she was not sure which order of the Court they are not complying with, that there has been a number of reasons why there was no joint trial plan, that they made three attempts to try and come up with a joint trial plan, that they could not agree and that she was not aware of what would happen if they could not agree. The Court informed the parties that it starts preparing for these matters way ahead of the hearing date, that it if the Court takes the time to prepare an order all parties need to understand that compliance is necessary and that if there is non-compliance, there will be forfeitures going forward. The Court also stated that the parties were to provide information to allow the petitions for probate to go forward and that all of the petitions for probate out for trial in Dept. 206. The Amended Petition for Probate of Lost Will and the Amended Petition for Probate are set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on March 8, 2021 at 9:30 a.m., (order for the same signed in open court). A copy of the order and notice of trial was provided to Ms. Hipps in open Court. Ms. Hipps is required to serve a copy of said order on all parties. The Second Amended Petition to Obtain Possession of Real and Personal Property, the Petition for Order Reforming, or Alternatively Modifying the Trust, etc. and the Petition to Remove Trustee Lawrence Porchia are continued to June 22, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petitioner Donald Trammel By And Through His Gal Clinton Killians Petition To Remove Trustee Lawrence Porchia And Appointment Of Clinton Killian As Trustee Ad Litem
Off Calendar Feb-23-2021 Continued To Jun-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petitioner Donald Trammel By And Through His Gal Clinton Killians Petition To Remove Trustee Lawrence Porchia And Appointment Of Clinton Killian As Trustee Ad Litem
Off Calendar Feb-02-2021 Continued To Feb-23-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Reforming, Or Alternately Modifying The Terms Of The Trust Or In The Alternative Modifying The Trust Or In The Alternative Seeking
Off Calendar Feb-02-2021 Continued To Feb-23-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-17-301488 Estate of Genette Trammell and
PTR-18-302129 The Genette Trammell Living Trust.

Appearances: Jennifer Brown and Jose Cordova appeared by zoom for and with Floyd Trammell (Petitioner) by zoom.
Jennifer Supman and Christina Morgan appeared by zoom for Vanguard Properties and Kilby Stenkamp (Respondents).
Nader Fannyan appeared by zoom for Clinton Killian (Guardian Ad Litem for Donald Trammell).
Kendall Layne appeared by zoom for Norcal Gold, Fusion Real Estate Network and Tracy Dearman (Respondents).
Christiane Hipps appeared in court for and with Lawrence Porchia in court.
Several witnesses (9) appeared in person and by zoom.

The Court stated that there is no joint trial plan, that it will not have a trial without a joint trial plan that is signed off by everyone and that the e-mails between Counsel have not been productive. The Court also stated that the parties got started too late and that they can't produce a joint product in such little time with this many parties. The Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (2), the Second Amended Petition to Obtain Possession of Real and Personal Property, the Petition for Order Reforming, or Alternately Modifying the Terms of the Trust, etc., the Petition to Remove Trustee, etc. and status of mediation are continued to February 23, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A joint trial plan is to be filed by no later than February 16, 2021. The Court informed Counsel not to bring witnesses on February 23rd and noted that with the amount of witnesses it looks like this may go out to Dept. 206 for trial. Ms. Brown stated that she thought Judge Quinn indicated that only the issue of the controlling document is to be tried. Ms. Hipps asked the Court not to hold Mr. Porchia in contempt. The Court will not find Lawrence Porchia in contempt and will not issue sanctions.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Reforming, Or Alternately Modifying The Terms Of The Trust Or In The Alternative Modifying The Trust Or In The Alternative Seeking
Off Calendar Oct-27-2020 Continued To Jan-12-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard: PES-17-301488 Estate of Genette Trammell and PTR-18-302129 The Genette Trammell Living Trust

Appearances: Chrstianne Hipps appeared by zoom for and with Lawrence Porchia (present in Court).
Jennifer Supman appeared by zoom for Vanguard Properties and Kilby Stenkamp (Real Estate Agent).
Jennifer Brown appeared by zoom for Floyd Trammell.
Christina Morgan appeared by zoom for Kilby Stenkamp (individually).
Kendall Layne appeared by telephone for Norcal Gold and Tracy Dearman.
Nader Fannyan appeared by zoom for Clinton Killian Guardian Ad Litem for Donald Trammell.
Scott Long appeared by telephone for Caroline Ingeborn and Gustav Lindqvist (Respondents).

The Court informed the Parties that the probate matters will be set for hearing, stated that the issues raised in the probate Examiner's notes with any of these petitions have not been addressed, and that if the issues are not cleared up by the next calling, the matters will go off calendar. The Court also stated that there is no will, that there is no proof of publication and there is no proposed order. Mr. Porchia stated that the original will was filed in 2018, and that there is a death certificate for Cheryl, but he doesn't have it with him. The Court spoke of the issue of 22 adults living in the Potomac property and stated that Mr. Porchia needs to exercise more due diligence to find addresses for the parties. Mr. Porchia stated that there are no new developments with mediation. Ms. Brown stated that she believes mediation is still in range mow that Mr. Porchia has Counsel. The Court stated that the issue of resolving the competing petitions may require an evidentiary hearing. Ms. Hipps stated that she would like time to respond to the issues raised by the Court and requested a continuance out to January. Ms. Brown stated that she will not be available at the end of January. The Court will issue a trial order for an evidentiary hearing on the two probate petitions. The evidentiary hearing is set for January 12, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to submit a joint trial plan and then the Court will determine if the matter will remain in Probate, or go out to the trial department. The Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (2), the Petition to Obtain Possession of Real and Personal Property, the Petition for Order Reforming, or Alternately Modifying the Terms of the Trust, etc., the Petition to Remove Trustee, etc. and status of mediation are continued to January 12, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Reforming, Or Alternately Modifying The Terms Of The Trust Or In The Alternative Modifying The Trust Or In The Alternative Seeking
Off Calendar Jul-16-2019 Continued To Sep-17-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-17-301488 Estate of Genette Trammell and PTR-18-302129 The Gennette Trammell Living Trust.

Appearances: Clinton Killian (Guardian Ad Litem) for Donald Trammell in Propria Persona.
Timothy Barteau specially appearing for Keystone Law Group as Counsel for and with Lawrence Porchia (Trustee).
Scott Long for Caroline Ingeborn and Gustav Lindqvist (Respondents).
Jennifer Supman appeared by telephone for Kilby Stenkamp and Vangaurd Properties.
Tanner Brink appeared by telephone for Chicago Title Company.
Kendall Layne for Tracy Dearman and Fusion Real Estate Network.

The Court stated that it recently ruled on the demurrers on the 850 petition which resulted in amending of the petition, that it is not sure what can be done at this time and that Mr. Trammell also filed a petition to remove the Trustee in the estate matter. Mr. Killian apologized, stated that he misunderstood and that he thought the matters were consolidated. The Petition to Remove Trustee, etc. is off calendar without prejudice to it being filed in the trust matter. Mr. Porchia stated that he is still searching for new Counsel. Discussion was held with regard to whether or not it is appropriate to set the petitions for probate for trial, whether it is premature to set them when Mr. Porchia is looking for new Counsel and whether they have been litigated enough for the point of setting them for trial. Mr. Killian stated that he will circulate a proposal. The Court reminds all parties that the concern should be on preservation of the estate for Donald Trammell and that they should all be aware of how much money is being spent litigation. The Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (2), the Petition to Obtain Possession of Real and Personal Property, the Petition for Order Reforming, or Alternately Modifying the Terms of the Trust, etc. and status of mediation are continued to September 17, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Conurt encourages the parties to meet and confer.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Jul-10-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-17-301488 Estate of Genette Trammell and PTR-18-302129 The Gennette Trammell Living Trust.

Appearances: Jennifer Brown for Floyd Trammell (Petitioner).
Nader Fannyan for Clinton Killian (Guardian Ad Litem for Donald Trammell).
Timothy Barteau for and with Lawrence Porchia.

Mr. Porchia stated that he is objecting to the motion. The Court asks Ms. Brown and Mr. Fannyan to exit the courtroom, and holds a closed hearing/in camera proceeding. Only Court Personnel, Mr. Barteau and Mr. Porchia are present in the courtroom. The Court hears oral argument. Upon conclusion of argument and assuming that Mr. Porchia is willing to sign a conflict waiver that is acceptable to both parties, the Court grants the Motions to Be Relieved as Counsel in both matters based on non-payment and Mr. Porchia's inability to pay, but will be effective after the July 16th hearing (subject to receipt and review of revised orders). The Court informed Mr. Porchia that this requires him to obtain other representation or work out an agreement with the current firm. Ms. Brown and Mr. Fannyan are called back into the courtroom and are advised of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Order Reforming, Or Alternately Modifying The Terms Of The Trust Or In The Alternative Modifying The Trust Or In The Alternative Seeking
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Continued To Apr-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-17-301488 Estate of Genette Trammell and PTR-18-302129 The Gennette Trammell Living Trust.

Appearances: Jennifer Brown for and with Floyd Trammell (Petitioner).
Clinton Killian Guardian Ad Litem for Donald Trammell (Sole Beneficiary).
Lindsey Munyer appeared by telephone for and with Lawrence Porchia (Successor Trustee present in Court).

Mr. Killian stated that two parties reached an agreement regarding who shall serve as the Executor and the Trustee, but there are substantive issues regarding sale of the property, that delay caused the estate to lose $500,000.00 in value, that there are over $500,000.00 in disbursements and payments that were paid by the Trustee to the detriment of the Beneficiary and that he has prepared an 850 Petition to recover $1,000,000.00. Ms. Munyer stated that she is hesitant to speak about the settlement agreement, that it is confidential and that it has not been memorialized. The Court stated that all it has before it is the competing petitions for probate and that it does not prevent the parties from asserting any claims. Ms. Munyer stated that she believes the competing petitions are settled, that the Guardian Ad Litem will not sign off on the agreement, that he was not part of mediation, that they do not have global settlement and requested that the Court set a mandatory settlement conference. Mr. Killian stated that the Guardian Ad Litem is neutral as to who serves, that the problem was when they found out that the property was sold without any notice and that they can not have any claims without having information. Mr. Killian also stated that the accounting was filed several months late and that it is not prudent for his Client to waive what appears to be a loss of $1,000,000.00. Ms. Brown stated that they reached an agreement for both parties to serve as Co-Administrators, and that the issues between Mr. Porchia and her Client are no longer and have been resolved. Mr. Killian stated that he has no objection to the parties being Co-Administrators, suggested continuing the competing petitions and stated that he will file a petition for appointment of a Special Administrator. The Court urges the parties to resolve what is before the Court, encouraged Mr. Killian to participate in anyway he can in broader settlement and encouraged the parties to get Mr. Killian what he needs. The Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, the Petition for Order Reforming, or Alternately Modifying the Terms of the Trust, etc. and status of mediation are continued to April 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Order Reforming, Or Alternately Modifying The Terms Of The Trust Or In The Alternative Modifying The Trust Or In The Alternative Seeking
Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Continued To Feb-19-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-17-301488 Estate of Genette Trammell and PTR-18-302129 The Genette Trammell Living Trust.

Appearances: Timothy Bartteau for and with Lawrence Porchia (Trustee).
Clinton Killian Guardian Ad Litem for Donald Trammell.
Jennifer Brown for and with Floyd Trammell.

Floyd Trammell stated that he thought they had an understanding, but it was not in writing. Mr. Barteau stated that they had a long round of mediation, that there is some issue as to the agreement, that he has contacted the Mediator to see if they can go back around it and that he has not heard back from the Mediator. Mr. Killian suggested a 30-day continuance to see if the parties can resolve this and if not, set the matter for hearing. The Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, and status on mediation are continued to February 19, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to submit a joint status statement no later than February 13, 2019. If the case is not resolved, the parties are to meet and confer as to what the actual issues are, how the matter is going to go forward and clearing up any procedural issues.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.
